image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Infrared wavelengths slip through dust clouds that block visible light.
Context visual: In space, dust is everywhere. It hides stars, galaxies, and even entire star-forming regions.
Escalation visual: Visible light gets absorbed or scattered, but infrared? It just passes through.
Evidence visual: Telescopes like the JWST use infrared to unveil the cosmos, revealing what we can’t see with our eyes.
Payoff visual: From ancient stellar nurseries to distant galaxies, infrared light expands our view of the universe.
Cta visual: Want to unlock more cosmic secrets? Follow us for more amazing astronomy insights!
